title: Not using volumes after Podman Machine restart to rootful?
body:
### Issue Description

After restarting the Podman Machine all the containers   were gone. After re-running the docker-compose file for the containers it restarts the containers but doesn't use the data from volumes.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Setup WordPress through official docker-compose and upload some files
2. Edit port from 8080:80 -> 80:80
3. Run` podman machine set --rootful` to enable access to 80 port
4. Restart Podman
5. Run the edited docker-compose


### Describe the results you received

Wordpress reset itself. Despite the files being inside the volume it started up to the setup page and doesn't detect installed plugins or uploaded files.

### Describe the results you expected

After restarting Podman and running the docker-compose with minor changes the old volume should be automatically used. Wordpress should be as I left it with the same files and  plugins except  running on a different port.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I am running docker inside Windows WSL(2).

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Based on the output I'm assuming that it's now putting things inside `/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes`? Is there a way to  have both root and non root use same folder?

I managed to get it working by using podman in normal mode and setting the WP container as `privileged:true` inside compose but it bothers me that they are seperate.

I find it odd that they have a completly seperate directory and  now don't show up when using `podman container list`. Is there a way to manage both privelaged and non privelaged?
It would be very difficult to use the same folder, because of permissions.  A rootless user would not be allowed to write to directories owned by root.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is not possible as @rhatdan said, I created https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20387 to document the effect of the --rootful option better.
